preview_text:
The model favors Japan to win at 71%, leaving Tunisia with a 8% chance of victory. Japan's dominance in the forecast reflects a substantial probability gap, though the underdog retains meaningful upside in a single-match context.

The win-draw-loss spread reveals a competitive dynamic weighted heavily toward Japan. A draw carries 21% probability from the model, suggesting the match carries real uncertainty within a Japan-favoring framework. Tunisia's path to victory remains narrow, but the draw outcome points to a contest unlikely to be entirely one-sided in execution.

The market consensus diverges notably from the model. Sportsbooks assign Japan only 56% win probability—15 percentage points lower than the model—while raising Tunisia's chances to 18% and keeping the draw near 26%. Polymarket prices align more closely with the books than the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate which forecast is more accurate; the gap merits consideration by users evaluating their own views.
